sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: {"system": "AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition (2017)", "t_stages": [{"stage": "TX", "description": "Primary tumor cannot be assessed"}, {"stage": "T0", "description": "No evidence of primary tumor"}, {"stage": "Tis", "description": "Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae)"}, {"stage": "T1", "description": "Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosae but not into the muscularis propria)"}, {"stage": "T2", "description": "Tumor invades the muscularis propria"}, {"stage": "T3", "description": "Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues"}, {"stage": "T4", "description": "Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum or invades or adheres to adjacent organ or structure"}, {"stage": "T4a", "description": "Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum)"}, {"stage": "T4b", "description": "Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures"}], "n_stages": [{"stage": "NX", "description": "Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed"}, {"stage": "N0", "description": "No regional lymph node metastasis"}, {"stage": "N1", "description": "One to three regional lymph nodes are positive (tumor in lymph nodes measuring ≥0.2 mm), or any number of tumor deposits are present and all identifiable lymph nodes are negative"}, {"stage": "N1a", "description": "One regional lymph node is positive"}, {"stage": "N1b", "description": "Two or three regional lymph nodes are positive"}, {"stage": "N1c", "description": "No regional lymph nodes are positive, but there are tumor deposits in the subserosa, mesentery, or nonperitonealized pericolic, or perirectal/mesorectal tissues"}, {"stage": "N2", "description": "Four or more regional lymph nodes are positive"}, {"stage": "N2a", "description": "Four to six regional lymph nodes are positive"}, {"stage": "N2b", "description": "Seven or more regional lymph nodes are positive"}], "m_stages": [{"stage": "M0", "description": "No distant metastasis by imaging, etc.; no evidence of tumor in distant sites or organs"}, {"stage": "M1", "description": "Metastasis to one or more distant sites or organs or peritoneal metastasis is identified"}, {"stage": "M1a", "description": "Metastasis to one site or organ is identified without peritoneal metastasis"}, {"stage": "M1b", "description": "Metastasis to two or more sites or organs is identified without peritoneal metastasis"}, {"stage": "M1c", "description": "Metastasis to the peritoneal surface is identified alone or with other site or organ metastases"}], "stage_groups": [{"group": "Stage 0", "criteria": "Tis, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Carcinoma in situ; confined to mucosa; no metastatic potential", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage I", "criteria": "T1, T2, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades submucosa or muscularis propria; no nodal involvement", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIA", "criteria": "T3, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor penetrates through muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues; node-negative", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIB", "criteria": "T4a, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor penetrates through visceral peritoneum; node-negative", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIC", "criteria": "T4b, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs/structures; node-negative", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIIA", "criteria": "T1-T2, N1/N1c, M0 OR T1, N2a, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Low T-stage with regional nodal involvement", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIIB", "criteria": "T3-T4a, N1/N1c, M0 OR T2-T3, N2a, M0 OR T1-T2, N2b, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Higher T-stage with regional nodal involvement or lower T-stage with extensive nodal involvement", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IIIC", "criteria": "T4a, N2a, M0 OR T3-T4a, N2b, M0 OR T4b, N1-N2, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Advanced local disease with extensive nodal involvement", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IVA", "criteria": "Any T, Any N, M1a", "clinical_meaning": "Metastasis to one site or organ without peritoneal metastasis", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Palliative with potential for cure in select resectable cases"}, {"group": "Stage IVB", "criteria": "Any T, Any N, M1b", "clinical_meaning": "Metastasis to two or more sites or organs without peritoneal metastasis", "five_yr_survival": null, "treatment_intent": "Palliative with potential for cure in select resectable cases"}]}

t_stages.description: Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae)
Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosae but not into the muscularis propria)
Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum or invades or adheres to adjacent organ or structure
Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum)
Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
One to three regional lymph nodes are positive (tumor in lymph nodes measuring ≥0.2 mm), or any number of tumor deposits are present and all identifiable lymph nodes are negative
One regional lymph node is positive
Two or three regional lymph nodes are positive
No regional lymph nodes are positive, but there are tumor deposits in the subserosa, mesentery, or nonperitonealized pericolic, or perirectal/mesorectal tissues
Four or more regional lymph nodes are positive
Four to six regional lymph nodes are positive
Seven or more regional lymph nodes are positive

m_stages.description: No distant metastasis by imaging, etc.; no evidence of tumor in distant sites or organs
Metastasis to one or more distant sites or organs or peritoneal metastasis is identified
Metastasis to one site or organ is identified without peritoneal metastasis
Metastasis to two or more sites or organs is identified without peritoneal metastasis
Metastasis to the peritoneal surface is identified alone or with other site or organ metastases
